title: Camellia pleurocarpa
intro: Camellia pleurocarpa, also known as the Japanese camellia or Taiwanese camellia, is a species of flowering plant in the tea family (Theaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of gastritis and ulcers, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Camellia pleurocarpa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities. In traditional Chinese medicine, Camellia pleurocarpa is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies such as tablets and capsules.
taxonomy: Camellia pleurocarpa is a species of flowering plant in the family Theaceae. It belongs to the genus Camellia, which is a group of evergreen shrubs and small trees native to Asia. The species is further classified into the subgenus Camellia, which contains about 100 species of Camellia. Camellia pleurocarpa is often grouped within the subsection Thea, which includes species with similar characteristics. Within the subsection Thea, Camellia pleurocarpa is considered a rare species native to China.
